paper_title: Ambrosia Pollen and Distribution Study Methods

methods.method_name: Ambrosia Pollen Monitoring and SPI Calculation
methods.description: Process for monitoring Ambrosia L. pollen concentration in the air using a volumetric method, determining the pollen season using the 98% method, and calculating a Seasonal Pollen Index (SPI).

steps.description: The pollen count in the air of Sosnowiec was measured by means of the volumetric method using a Burkard spore trap (Hirsttype).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure airborne pollen concentration using a volumetric spore trap.

steps.description: The beginning and the end of the pollen season was determined by means of the 98% method.
steps.searchable_summary: Define pollen season start and end dates using the 98% cumulative count method.
inputs: Daily pollen counts over a period

steps.description: To compare the pollen counts over the studied years, a Seasonal Pollen Index (SPI) was calculated as the sum of the daily pollen counts in a given season.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the Seasonal Pollen Index (SPI) by summing daily counts within the defined season.

methods.description: Acquisition of meteorological data from a nearby weather station and atmospheric circulation data for assessing their impact on pollen seasons.

steps.description: The average daily values of air temperature, relative humidity, precipitation, insolation, wind speed and direction were taken into account to assess the impact of meteorological parameters on selected characteristics of the pollen seasons.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain daily average meteorological data (temperature, humidity, precipitation, insolation, wind) from a weather station.
outputs: Daily meteorological parameters (temperature, humidity, precipitation, insolation, wind speed, wind direction)

steps.description: Furthermore, the direction and type of atmospheric circulation were subjected to analysis (N i e d ź w i e d ź , 1981).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain data on atmospheric circulation patterns (direction, type).
inputs: Atmospheric circulation data source (e.g., reanalysis data, classification scheme)

methods.description: Statistical analysis using Spearman's rank correlation coefficient to determine the relationships between individual meteorological parameters and various characteristics of the pollen season.

steps.description: The relationships between individual meteorological parameters and various characteristics of the pollen season in all years were determined by means of Spearman's rank correlation coefficient (S t a n i s z , 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze relationships between meteorological variables and pollen season characteristics using Spearman's rank correlation.
inputs: Pollen season characteristics (e.g., SPI, season length, peak date)

methods.method_name: Ambrosia Distribution Data Collection and Mapping
methods.description: Comprehensive data collection on the historical and current distribution of Ambrosia species, particularly A. artemisiifolia, from various sources and mapping the distribution using the cartogram method.

steps.description: The data about the history of the appearance and further spread of the species of the Ambrosia genus in the Silesian Uplands area (especially A. artemisiifolia) come from original observations and all relevant published, unpublished and herbarium data obtained by other researchers (T o k a r s k a -G u z i k , 2005; T o k a r s k a -G u z i k et al. 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plant distribution data from diverse sources including observations, publications, and herbaria.

steps.description: The unpublished materials sent to the database "Distribution Atlas of Vascular Plants in Poland -ATPOL" (Z a j ą c and Z a j ą c , 2001) by botanists from all over Poland... provided the most essential and the most exten-sive information about the distribution of the species.
steps.searchable_summary: Access and extract plant distribution data from the ATPOL national database, including unpublished contributions.

steps.description: ...the data gathered on the basis of our own floristic surveys provided the most essential and the most exten-sive information about the distribution of the species.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect plant distribution data through field surveys.
outputs: Plant occurrence records from field surveys

steps.description: A detailed list of the sites of the species examined can be found in the database of the program which gathers information about the distribution of alien naturalized species, the so-called neophytes or kenophytes (AT-POL-KENO).The program is an integral part of the national database of ATPOL.
steps.searchable_summary: Access and extract data on alien naturalized species distribution from the AT-POL-KENO database.
outputs: Alien species occurrence records from AT-POL-KENO

steps.description: These data were gathered for the Silesian Uplands, as for the whole country, by means of the cartogram method (Fig. 2).However, in this case a square with a side of 2 kilometres was a basic unit (the data for Poland are gathered in squares with a side of 10 kilometres (T o k a r s k a -G u z i k et al. 2011; Z a j ą c , 1978; Z a j ą c and Z a j ą c , 2001).
steps.searchable_summary: Aggregate plant distribution data into grid cells (cartogram units) of 2 km x 2 km for a specific region.
inputs: Definition of the Silesian Uplands region
outputs: Gridded distribution data (e.g., presence/absence or count per grid cell)

steps.description: A map of the distribution of A. artemisiifolia in Poland was published in "Distribution Atlas of Vascular Plants in Poland" (T o k a rs k a -G u z i k , 2001).The map shown here contains further additions (Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Create a map visualizing the distribution of A. artemisiifolia based on gridded data.
